name: Wendell Marshall
abstract: Wendell Lewis Marshall (October 24, 1920 – February 6, 2002) was an American jazz double-bassist. Marshall was Jimmy Blanton's cousin.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Thanksgiving Jazz at Carnegie Hall

about_quotes.speaker: Jones Hank
about_quotes.evidence: You did all the stuff with Wendell Marshall and Kenny Clarke... We had Wendell Marshall on bass usually... I was the pianist.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jones Hank — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kenny Clarke
about_quotes.evidence: "Hank and Wendell Marshall, that's my rhythm section... we went out to Rudy Van Gelder's and made the first Cannonball date."

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder
about_quotes.evidence: I got off the train with Wendell Marshall, Jimmy Blanton’s cousin – first cousin. He was my roommate.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Frishberg
about_quotes.evidence: It was — the drummer was Osie Johnson and the bass player was Wendell Marshall. I played piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Frishberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 16, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Duke Ellington's band. This was a great band, this particular band was a great band. And this was -- band was Louie Bellson, Wendell Marshall, I believe, Shorty Baker, Cooke, what's his name.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: And you're on the record; the people on the record are ... Juan Tizol ... and Wendell Marshall ... I played it last night. Do you remember that? ... He was another nice bass player, too.
